researchRationale: Shared knee-flexion and extensor-demand contexts; direct target outputs remain separate.
Complex context may bridge sparse cartilage evidence without merging tissue-specific claims.
ACL and PCL are searched together but values are never merged.
Complex demand may describe coronal or rotational control; specific ligament values require direct or validated evidence.
Tissue mechanical load and dynamic stabilization are parallel profiles.
Propulsion and impact share ankle-angle and force-time context.
Passive ligament or joint load remains separate from dynamic peroneal stabilization.
External-rotation and dorsiflexion context may be complex-level; direct structure claims remain separate.
Arch and peritalar context may be complex-level; FDL and FHL require explicit support.
